description: Curated fixes to V2.9.1 extracted message structure data. Applied by tooling/scripts/apply_v291_fixes.py to produce canonical message structures in v291-canonical/message-structures/.
methodology: Fixes are identified by comparing multiple occurrences of the same message structure ID across V2.9.1 Word documents. Where occurrences disagree, the majority/consensus value is used. For single-occurrence structures, fixes are based on pattern analysis (e.g., bracket mismatch reconstruction).

fixes.reasoning: Normalize all bracket notation to standard forms: [ SEG ] (optional), { SEG } (repeating), [{ SEG }] (optional+repeating), SEG (required). Whitespace-only change, no semantic impact.
Extra closing brace. All other ADT_A01 occurrences have [{ OH2 }].
Closing brace should be bracket. Consensus from other ADT_A01 occurrences is [ OH3 ] (optional, non-repeating).
Extra closing brace. Consensus from other occurrences is [{ PRT }].
Misspelling of 'Adverse'. All other ADT_A01 occurrences spell it correctly.
Extra closing brace. Other ADT_A05 occurrences have [{ PRT }].
Extra closing brace. Other ADT_A05 occurrences have [{ IAM }].
Extra closing brace. Other ADT_A06 occurrence has [{ OH2 }].
Both ADT_A06 occurrences have this same malformed bracket. Closing brace should be bracket. OH3 in other ADT structures (A01, A05) is optional non-repeating [ OH3 ].
Extra closing brace. PRT is consistently [{ PRT }] across the standard.
Extra closing brace. Other ADT_A09 occurrences have [{ PRT }].
Extra closing brace. Single occurrence but consistent with PRT pattern across all structures.
Extra closing brace. Single occurrence but consistent with PRT pattern.
Extra closing brace. 8 of 9 ADT_A21 occurrences have [{ PRT }].
Missing opening brace and malformed closing. REL is optional+repeating throughout the standard.
Extra closing bracket instead of brace. NTE is optional+repeating.
Same as FIX-0014. REL is optional+repeating.
Same as FIX-0014. REL is optional+repeating.
Same as FIX-0015. Extra closing bracket instead of brace.
Extra closing bracket instead of brace. ARV is optional+repeating.
Extra closing bracket instead of brace. Other ORU_R30 occurrences have [{ OBX }].
Missing closing brackets entirely. NK1 in similar structures is optional+repeating.
Misspelling of 'Present'. All other ORU_R01 occurrences spell it correctly.
Trailing open parenthesis artifact. All other MDM_T01 occurrences have 'Event Type' without trailing paren.

Standardize all ACK segment descriptions to title case. Affected: "Software segment" → "Software Segment" (7 occurrences), "Message header" → "Message Header" (1), "Message acknowledgment" → "Message Acknowledgment" (1).

MSH segment description should be "Message Header", not "Message Acknowledgment". 3 ACK occurrences (from CH05, CH15) incorrectly label MSH as "Message Acknowledgment". The remaining 112 occurrences correctly say "Message Header".
MSA description should be "Message Acknowledgment", not just "Acknowledgment". 10 ACK occurrences use the shortened form; the remaining 105 use the full form.
SFT description should be "Software", not "Software Segment". The segment name is "Software"; trailing "Segment" is redundant. 22 occurrences already use "Software".
UAC description should be "User Authentication Credential", not "User Authentication Credential Segment". 104 occurrences already use the shorter form.
ERR description should be "Error". 102 occurrences use "Error"; 13 use "Error Information". The segment name is ERR (Error).
ERR should be optional+repeating [{ ERR }] in ACK. This clause has [ ERR ] (optional, non-repeating). 111 of 115 ACK occurrences have [{ ERR }].
Same as FIX-0030. ERR should be [{ ERR }] in ACK.
Same as FIX-0030. ERR should be [{ ERR }] in ACK.
Same as FIX-0030. ERR should be [{ ERR }] in ACK.

Clause 10.4 (ACK^S12-S24,S26,S27) has UAC as [{ UAC }] (optional+repeating), while all other 114 ACK occurrences have [ UAC ] (optional, non-repeating). Is UAC repeating intentional for scheduling acknowledgments, or a typo? Needs V2 Management review before applying.

Clause number mis-parsed from Word document XML. The QBP^Znn^QBP_Q11 table is in clause 5.3.3.2 per the published standard. Additionally, segment descriptions appear as bracket notation echoes (e.g., desc='[{ARV}]' instead of 'Access Restrictions') — likely caused by hidden columns or Word XML formatting artifacts. The structure (segments column) is correct and identical to all other QBP_Q11 occurrences. Exclude from multi-occurrence description comparison.

Second table in clause 14.3.2 (tableIndex 3) has caption 'ACK^N02^ACK' in the Word document, but the extractor reused the previous caption 'NMD^N02^NMD_N02' because it did not detect the new heading. The 5-row structure (MSH, [{SFT}], [UAC], MSA, [{ERR}]) is a standard ACK pattern. Reclassify as structureId=ACK (message ACK^N02), consistent with all other ACK variants.
Misspelling of 'Participation'. All other ADT_A05 occurrences spell it correctly.

fixes.from: Sex Parameter for Clinical UseSex Parameter for Clinical Use
fixes.reasoning: Doubled text — description was duplicated in Word document extraction. Other RDE_O11 occurrence has the correct single text.

Remove trailing ' Segment' from segment descriptions. Description should name the segment type, not include the word 'Segment' (e.g., 'Software' not 'Software Segment', 'Message Header' not 'Message Header Segment'). Applies across all structures.
Replace en-dashes (U+2013) and em-dashes (U+2014) with regular hyphens in segment descriptions. Standardize on ASCII dash for consistency.
ROL segment description should be 'Role', not 'Deprecated as of V2.9'. The deprecation status is conveyed through the status column, not the description. All other ADT_A05 occurrences use 'Role'.

PRT in the COMMON_ORDER group. Description should match group name with titleized words: 'Participation (for Common Order)'. The reference occurrence at 7.3.1 says 'Participation (for Observation)' which is incorrect — this PRT is in the COMMON_ORDER group, not the OBSERVATION group. The 7.3.12 occurrence correctly identifies the group but needs titleization.

PRT at row 44 in ORU_R01 is in the COMMON_ORDER group, not the OBSERVATION group. Fix the reference occurrence (7.3.1) to match the corrected description. Clause 7.3.12 correctly identified this as common order.
Same as FIX-0040. PRT at row 44 is in the COMMON_ORDER group, not the OBSERVATION group.
Same as FIX-0040. PRT at row 44 is in the COMMON_ORDER group, not the OBSERVATION group.
Remove lowercase 'segment' suffix from MFA description. 13 of 17 occurrences use 'Master File ACK'; 4 have 'Master File ACK segment'.

fixes.from: For backwards compatibility only as of V2.9.
fixes.to: For backwards compatibility only as of V2.9
fixes.reasoning: Remove trailing period from ARV description. 2 of 4 occurrences have the trailing period; standardize without it.
Group names use ALL_CAPS convention. 3 of 4 ADT_A05 occurrences have 'PROCEDURE'.
Missing space after '---' in group marker. All other group markers have a space.

Missing space before 'Cert' after dash, plus en-dash normalization. Other RQI_I01 occurrence has 'Insurance Add'l Info - Cert' with proper spacing. The from value uses the raw Word characters (en-dash, smart quote) before BULK-003 normalization.
Misspelling of 'Participation'.
Misspelling of 'Container'.
Misspelling of 'Specimen'.
Misspelling of 'Observation'.
Same misspelling of 'Observation' as FIX-0051.
Missing closing parenthesis.
Missing closing parenthesis.
Titleize 'common order' in PRT description. Affects OPU_R25, OUL_R22, OUL_R23, OUL_R24.
Titleize 'observation' in PRT description. Lowercase form appears in several structures.
Standardize to American spelling 'Acknowledgment' (without 'e'). The reclassified NMD_N02 ACK response uses British spelling 'Acknowledgement'.
OBX in the PATIENT_OBSERVATION group should use the group name pattern 'Patient Observation', not 'Observations on Patient'.
ARV is [{ ARV }] (repeating) — plural form 'Access Restrictions' matches repeating cardinality. Repeating segments get plural descriptions.
Same as FIX-0057. ARV is repeating, should use plural.
Same as FIX-0057. ARV is repeating, should use plural.
RCP is required, non-repeating — singular form 'Response Control Parameter' matches cardinality. Non-repeating segments get singular descriptions.
Same as FIX-0060. RCP is non-repeating, should use singular.
QPD segment name is 'Query Parameter Definition'. The abbreviated form 'Query Definition Segment' appears in only 1 of 7 RTB_K13 occurrences. Also strips 'Segment' suffix.
Pluralize group name: COMPONENT → COMPONENTS. The group is repeating ([{...}]) in all pharmacy structures, meaning multiple components can be present. Plural matches semantics.
Matching end marker for BULK-006.

Replace segment code 'ORC' with preceding segment description 'Common Order'. All 12 instances are preceded by ORC segment described as 'Common Order'. Affects ORP_O10, RAS_O17, RDE_O11, RDS_O13, RGV_O15, RRA_O18, RRD_O14, RRE_O12, RRG_O16, RSP_K31, VXU_V04.

Replace segment code 'RXO' with segment description 'Pharmacy/Treatment Order'. 6 instances. NOTE: RDE_O11 (clauses 4A.4.6, 4A.4.16) has RXO described as 'Pharmacy/Treatment Prescription Order' (with extra 'Prescription') — a variant from the standard RXO description. RDS_O13 and RGV_O15 have a typo 'Pharmacy /Treatment Order' (extra space). Using the standard form here.

Replace segment code 'RXE' with preceding segment description 'Pharmacy/Treatment Encoded Order'. All 6 instances are preceded by RXE with this description. Affects RDE_O11, RDS_O13, RRE_O12, RSP_K31.
Replace segment code 'RXD' with preceding segment description 'Pharmacy/Treatment Dispense'. 1 instance in RSP_K31.
Replace segment code 'RXV' with preceding segment description 'Pharmacy/Treatment Infusion Order'. 2 instances in RDE_O11.

Replace segment code 'OBX' with group name 'Observation'. 5 of 6 instances are in the OBSERVATION group. NOTE: OML_O39 (clause 4.4.12) is in SHIPMENT_OBSERVATION group — 'for Observation' is still an improvement over 'for OBX' but a more precise qualifier would be 'for Shipment Observation'. Flagged for V2 Management review.
